codeReview.summary: The feature correctly adds { passive: true } to the identified scroll, resize, and touch listeners that do not call preventDefault(), and it adds .catch() handlers to both fire-and-forget PostHog import chains in use-analytics.ts. The worker also correctly excluded listeners that already had passive: true or rely on preventDefault().

issues.description: The commit includes several unrelated Tailwind class-ordering changes in this file in addition to the touchend passive-listener update. They appear cosmetic only, but they are out of scope for this feature review.
The commit includes an unrelated Tailwind class-ordering change on this line alongside the resize passive-listener update. Cosmetic only, but outside the requested feature scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker's commit bundled cosmetic Tailwind class-ordering diffs with the requested passive-listener changes. Mission guidance does not currently say whether formatter-only noise should be avoided or accepted when delivering narrowly scoped refactors.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show beff112d6ac22843ad82192390535c7abecef9c5 shows unrelated class-ordering hunks in packages/ui/primitives/multiselect.tsx and packages/ui/components/document/envelope-recipient-field-tooltip.tsx

summary: PASS. The passive-listener and PostHog .catch() requirements are implemented correctly. I found two non-blocking scope issues: unrelated cosmetic Tailwind class-ordering changes were included in multiselect.tsx and envelope-recipient-field-tooltip.tsx.
